report_metadata.step_description: CRITICAL OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: Every entry in the 'processing_plan' array MUST include a 'document_category' field set to exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. This field is mandatory — omitting it will cause step failure. Read the Step 1 document review output file and the tool registry. For each identified document produce a processing plan entry with ALL of the following fields: 'filename', 'document_type', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'recommended_reader', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'conversion_type', 'extraction_method', 'processing_notes', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. For spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) with document_category 'TAL', the processing plan entry will also contain 'worksheet_count' (total sheets in the file), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (non-empty sheets), and 'worksheets' (array of {sheet_name, row_count, column_count}) — these are pre-populated by the agent and must be preserved exactly as provided in the output. To assign 'document_category', apply these rules IN ORDER: RULE 0 - TAL SPREADSHEET: if the file extension is .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb, set document_category to 'TAL' regardless of any other rules. RULE 1 — SITE PLAN FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'RETIC', 'retic', 'DWG', 'dwg', 'SITEPLAN', 'DRAWING', 'SUBSTATION', set document_category to 'Site Plan'. RULE 2 — DESIGN BRIEF FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'BRIEF', 'brief', 'ARP', 'DESIGN_BRIEF', 'ARCHITECTURAL', 'SERVICES', 'TOWN_PLANNING', set document_category to 'Design Brief'. RULE 3 — CONTENT FALLBACK (only when filename has no clear signal): use document_type from Step 1 — 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', or 'Network Diagram' maps to 'Site Plan'; 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', or 'Town Planning Report' maps to 'Design Brief'; 'TAL' maps to 'TAL'. PAGE SIZE AND CHUNKING ASSESSMENT: Open each document and inspect the actual page dimensions. Classify page_size as one of: 'A4' (210Ã—297mm), 'A3' (297Ã—420mm), 'A2' (420Ã—594mm), 'A1' (594Ã—841mm), 'A0' (841Ã—1189mm), or 'large-format' (anything larger). Set requires_chunking to true if page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format' — these pages are too large for a single LLM context pass and must be split. For requires_chunking true, set chunk_strategy to 'quadrant-split' for A2/A1/A0/large-format drawings (split page into overlapping quadrants), or 'page-split' for multi-page text documents where individual pages exceed A3. Set estimated_chunks to the expected number of chunks per page (A2â†’4, A1â†’6, A0â†’9, large-formatâ†’12; use 1 for A4/A3). Set chunk_strategy to 'none' and estimated_chunks to 1 when requires_chunking is false. Example output entry: {"filename": "D526458_ARP4983_RETIC.pdf", "document_type": "Reticulation Drawing", "content_type": "image", "text_quality": "none", "recommended_reader": "ocr", "processing_tool_id": "tool-extract-pdf-content", "conversion_required": true, "conversion_type": "pdf-to-png", "extraction_method": "ocr", "processing_notes": "A1 large-format drawing, requires OCR with quadrant chunking", "document_category": "Site Plan", "page_size": "A1", "requires_chunking": true, "chunk_strategy": "quadrant-split", "estimated_chunks": 6} ROUTING RULES (set route_to_step in every processing plan entry): TAL files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) set route_to_step=6, requires_chunking=false, chunk_strategy="none" — they bypass Steps 3, 4, and 5 and go directly to Step 6 (asset spreadsheet extraction). Design Brief files set route_to_step=4. Site Plan files set route_to_step=5. Add "route_to_step" as a mandatory field in every processing plan entry.
report_metadata.validation_criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'processing_plan' key (array) and 'total_documents' integer. MANDATORY: every entry in 'processing_plan' must contain ALL of these fields: 'filename', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'extraction_method', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. The 'document_category' field must be exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. Files with RETIC/DWG/SITEPLAN/DRAWING/SUBSTATION in their filename must have document_category 'Site Plan'. Files with BRIEF/ARP/DESIGN_BRIEF/ARCHITECTURAL/SERVICES/TOWN_PLANNING in their filename must have document_category 'Design Brief'. The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. The 'requires_chunking' field must be true for any entry where page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When requires_chunking is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' (not 'none') and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1. Files with extension .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb must have document_category 'TAL'. TAL entries must also include 'worksheet_count' (integer), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (integer), and 'worksheets' (array). A missing or null 'document_category' is a critical failure. Every entry must also include 'route_to_step' (integer: 4 for Design Brief, 5 for Site Plan, 6 for TAL). TAL entries must have requires_chunking=false and chunk_strategy="none".

execution_context.prompt:
## STEP NAME
Plan document processing approach

## STEP DESCRIPTION
CRITICAL OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: Every entry in the 'processing_plan' array MUST include a 'document_category' field set to exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. This field is mandatory — omitting it will cause step failure. Read the Step 1 document review output file and the tool registry. For each identified document produce a processing plan entry with ALL of the following fields: 'filename', 'document_type', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'recommended_reader', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'conversion_type', 'extraction_method', 'processing_notes', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. For spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) with document_category 'TAL', the processing plan entry will also contain 'worksheet_count' (total sheets in the file), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (non-empty sheets), and 'worksheets' (array of {sheet_name, row_count, column_count}) — these are pre-populated by the agent and must be preserved exactly as provided in the output. To assign 'document_category', apply these rules IN ORDER: RULE 0 - TAL SPREADSHEET: if the file extension is .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb, set document_category to 'TAL' regardless of any other rules. RULE 1 — SITE PLAN FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'RETIC', 'retic', 'DWG', 'dwg', 'SITEPLAN', 'DRAWING', 'SUBSTATION', set document_category to 'Site Plan'. RULE 2 — DESIGN BRIEF FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'BRIEF', 'brief', 'ARP', 'DESIGN_BRIEF', 'ARCHITECTURAL', 'SERVICES', 'TOWN_PLANNING', set document_category to 'Design Brief'. RULE 3 — CONTENT FALLBACK (only when filename has no clear signal): use document_type from Step 1 — 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', or 'Network Diagram' maps to 'Site Plan'; 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', or 'Town Planning Report' maps to 'Design Brief'; 'TAL' maps to 'TAL'. PAGE SIZE AND CHUNKING ASSESSMENT: Open each document and inspect the actual page dimensions. Classify page_size as one of: 'A4' (210Ã—297mm), 'A3' (297Ã—420mm), 'A2' (420Ã—594mm), 'A1' (594Ã—841mm), 'A0' (841Ã—1189mm), or 'large-format' (anything larger). Set requires_chunking to true if page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format' — these pages are too large for a single LLM context pass and must be split. For requires_chunking true, set chunk_strategy to 'quadrant-split' for A2/A1/A0/large-format drawings (split page into overlapping quadrants), or 'page-split' for multi-page text documents where individual pages exceed A3. Set estimated_chunks to the expected number of chunks per page (A2â†’4, A1â†’6, A0â†’9, large-formatâ†’12; use 1 for A4/A3). Set chunk_strategy to 'none' and estimated_chunks to 1 when requires_chunking is false. Example output entry: {"filename": "D526458_ARP4983_RETIC.pdf", "document_type": "Reticulation Drawing", "content_type": "image", "text_quality": "none", "recommended_reader": "ocr", "processing_tool_id": "tool-extract-pdf-content", "conversion_required": true, "conversion_type": "pdf-to-png", "extraction_method": "ocr", "processing_notes": "A1 large-format drawing, requires OCR with quadrant chunking", "document_category": "Site Plan", "page_size": "A1", "requires_chunking": true, "chunk_strategy": "quadrant-split", "estimated_chunks": 6} ROUTING RULES (set route_to_step in every processing plan entry): TAL files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) set route_to_step=6, requires_chunking=false, chunk_strategy="none" — they bypass Steps 3, 4, and 5 and go directly to Step 6 (asset spreadsheet extraction). Design Brief files set route_to_step=4. Site Plan files set route_to_step=5. Add "route_to_step" as a mandatory field in every processing plan entry.

## VALIDATION CRITERIA
Must return a JSON object with a 'processing_plan' key (array) and 'total_documents' integer. MANDATORY: every entry in 'processing_plan' must contain ALL of these fields: 'filename', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'extraction_method', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. The 'document_category' field must be exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. Files with RETIC/DWG/SITEPLAN/DRAWING/SUBSTATION in their filename must have document_category 'Site Plan'. Files with BRIEF/ARP/DESIGN_BRIEF/ARCHITECTURAL/SERVICES/TOWN_PLANNING in their filename must have document_category 'Design Brief'. The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. The 'requires_chunking' field must be true for any entry where page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When requires_chunking is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' (not 'none') and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1. Files with extension .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb must have document_category 'TAL'. TAL entries must also include 'worksheet_count' (integer), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (integer), and 'worksheets' (array). A missing or null 'document_category' is a critical failure. Every entry must also include 'route_to_step' (integer: 4 for Design Brief, 5 for Site Plan, 6 for TAL). TAL entries must have requires_chunking=false and chunk_strategy="none".

## YOUR TASK
Generate and execute structured test cases against the agent's output. Use the FULL FILE CONTENTS (when provided) as your primary evidence. Cross-reference every item between input and output files. For each test case, document: the input data examined, the expected output, the actual output found, step-by-step execution notes of how you validated, and your final reasoning. Respond with the JSON test report.
